'''Locap''' was a type of [[plant]] found on the [[planet]] [[Naboo]] that harvested [[plasma]] from deep [[water]] porous rocks. The [[Gungan]] [[species]] discovered locap and learned to harvest stable plasma from the plant's buds using [[Tribubble bongo|bongos]], allowing them to use it as an energy source.

==Description==
Locap was a strange type of [[plant]] that could be found in the deep [[water]]s of the [[planet]] [[Naboo]]. The plant's roots burrowed into porous rock and siphoned out [[plasma]], which was then naturally stabilized by the locap's digestive processes. The stable plasma then collected in buds found at the tip of locap's stalks.<ref name="CL 2016" />

The buds had circular lobes with spiny [[Tooth|teeth]] that snap shut when any pressure is felt. The plants grew extremely slowly and lived for [[Standard century|centuries]].<ref name="CL 2016" /> Locap plants also had gigantic [[Color|brown]] branches, large enough for [[humanoid]] scale [[City|cities]] to be built on top.<ref name="TPM" />

==History==
[[File:Otoh_Gunga_on_locap.png|thumb|left|250px|Otoh Gunga was built atop locap branches.]]
Thousands of [[Standard year|years]] prior to [[32 BBY]], the [[Sentience|sentient]] [[Gungan]] [[species]] native to Naboo were forced underwater in a fierce struggle for territory and discovered locap. They were able to harvest plasma from the plant's buds using [[Tribubble bongo|bongos]] equipped with front-mounted siphons, then used the natural energy source to power much of their technology, including their underwater [[City|cities]]. Farming locap was a dangerous job due to the bud's teeth.<ref name="CL 2016" />

The [[city]] of [[Otoh Gunga]], anchored to a cliff located deep within [[Lake Paonga]], had [[hydrostatic bubble]]s that sat upon locap branches, including the [[City Bigspace]].<ref name="CL 2016" />

==Behind the scenes==
Locap first appeared in the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="TPM" /> It was first identified in the current [[canon]] in the [[2016]] reference book [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2016)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']].<ref name="CL 2016" /> Its name originated in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, where it was first mentioned in the [[2000]] reference book ''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Episode I]]'', which was written by [[Kristin Lund]].<ref name="Inside">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Episode I]]''</ref>
